title: Awards or Prizes
shortDescription: Documentation of the beneficiary's receipt of nationally or internationally recognized prizes or awards for excellence in the field of endeavor.
notes: No known awards. Not actively targeted.

title: Membership in Associations
shortDescription: Documentation of the beneficiary's membership in associations in the field which require outstanding achievements of their members, as judged by recognized national or international experts.

title: Published Material About the Applicant
shortDescription: Published material in professional or major trade publications or major media about the beneficiary, relating to the beneficiary's work in the field.
notes: PLANNED: Use HARO and Qwoted to get quoted by journalists on AI topics. Publish thought leadership on LLMs for enterprise, RAG in production, multi-agent system design. No outreach sent yet.

title: Judging the Work of Others
shortDescription: Evidence of the beneficiary's participation on a panel, or individually, as a judge of the work of others in the same or an allied field of specialization.
notes:
HIGHEST PRIORITY criterion being built.

ENROLLED: CVPR 2026, ICLR 2026 workshops.
APPLIED (awaiting): ACL ARR, COLM 2026, UAI 2026, TMLR, ACM CAIS.
DRAFTS READY: PAAMS 2026, ICML SRAS Workshop, Applied Intelligence (Springer), IJCAI-ECAI, JAIR, IEEE TNNLS, ACM TIST, AIJ, KDD workshops.
FUTURE: NeurIPS 2026 (May), ICML workshops (Apr).

Strategy: Journal > Conference workshop in USCIS officers' eyes. TMLR, JAIR, AIJ, IEEE TNNLS are highest value.
Differentiator: Industry practitioner framing — deploying production agentic systems at Walmart scale.

title: Original Contributions of Major Significance
shortDescription: Evidence of the beneficiary's original scientific, scholarly, artistic, athletic, or business-related contributions of major significance in the field.
notes: Potentially supportable: "Marty" — Walmart's first Agentic AI assistant for Marketplace Sellers (LangGraph, LangChain, FastAPI, Postgres, Redis, SSE streaming). Could be framed as an industry-first original contribution. Not formally documented yet.

title: Authorship of Scholarly Articles
shortDescription: Evidence of the beneficiary's authorship of scholarly articles in professional or major trade publications or other major media.
notes:
EXISTING: "Supervised Question Answering System for Technical Support" — IEEE ICMLA 2017 (DOI: 10.1109/ICMLA.2017.00-60). ~2 citations. Paper NOT yet indexed on OpenReview — needs to be claimed via Semantic Scholar.

ACTION PLAN: Write survey articles on arXiv/ResearchGate. Target IEEE Transactions, top NLP venues. Topics: LLMs for enterprise, RAG in production, multi-agent system design.

title: Artistic Exhibitions or Showcases
shortDescription: Evidence of the display of the beneficiary's work in the field at artistic exhibitions or showcases.
notes: N/A — not applicable to software engineering / ML field.

title: Leading or Critical Role
shortDescription: Evidence that the beneficiary has performed in a leading or critical role for organizations or establishments that have a distinguished reputation.
notes: Potentially supportable: Staff SWE at Walmart Global Tech, leading the Marty agentic AI project. Could argue critical role for a Fortune 1 company. Not formally pursued yet.

title: High Salary or Remuneration
shortDescription: Evidence that the beneficiary has commanded a high salary or other significantly high remuneration for services in relation to others in the field.
notes: QUALIFIES — Strong. Staff SWE compensation at Walmart Global Tech. Considered a strong, defensible criterion. No additional action needed; documentation required at filing time.

title: Commercial Success in Performing Arts
shortDescription: Evidence of commercial successes in the performing arts, as shown by box office receipts or record, cassette, compact disk, or video sales.
